reason: Geopolitical tensions from the Iran war are causing a significant 'risk-off' environment across global stock markets, putting considerable pressure on Bitcoin. While Bitcoin is 'attempting to make a stand,' its ability to hold critical support levels is being tested. Long-term fundamental news like increased mining capacity and AI integration into blockchain are positive but overshadowed by immediate macro risks. The Aave governance rift adds specific uncertainty to the DeFi sector. Extreme caution is warranted as the market seeks direction amidst high volatility.
headlines: AI agents will be primary users of blockchain, NEAR co-founder says
Aave governance rift deepens as major governance group exits $26 billion DeFi protocol
Eric Trump’s American Bitcoin buys 11,298 ASIC miners, increasing mining capacity by 12%
Bank of Japan expands blockchain settlement sandbox and says CBDC efforts are ongoing
Bitcoin attempting to make a stand as global stock markets melt down on Iran war
